mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.4% suggests room for further easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors frontrun recession fears.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.7% is near cyclical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal demand momentum. When both series show month-over-month growth acceleration simultaneously, it indicates robust aggregate demand that supports equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST show positive MoM acceleration in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 523514M and HOUST at 1253K suggest moderate demand
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint acceleration signals are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 1.5% over a 3-month rolling window, signaling supply-side weakness or demand destruction that compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 109.1756 reflects recent production levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative production signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month over a 3-month rolling average, signaling labor market softening that precedes equity market weakness. This mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal without discrete threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 151431K reflects strong employment
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods of sub-100K monthly job growth are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 3%, a real rate compression occurs that signals monetary accommodation. This joint condition produces positive equity returns over 60 trading days as investors seek real asset hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 256.161 and FEDFUNDS at 2.4% suggest accommodative real rates
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during joint accommodation signals are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate over a 2-quarter window, signaling economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 19023.82B reflects recent economic activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite sub-1.5% growth signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both show positive month-over-month growth simultaneously, indicating synchronized supply and labor expansion that supports equity valuations. This interaction-driven mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and PAYEMS show positive MoM growth in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 109.1756 and PAYEMS at 151431K suggest economic momentum
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following joint expansion signals are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 18-month window.
